steps.title: Identify what the blank needs
steps.content: The sentence reads: “Scientists have figured out why ______” The blank completes an embedded indirect question. After “why,” the clause must use normal subject-verb order (not inverted) and the full sentence must end with a period, not a question mark.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'is Uranus a different shade of blue than Neptune?': inverted verb-subject order (“is Uranus”) is used in direct questions, not indirect ones. The question mark also makes the full sentence a direct question, which contradicts “Scientists have figured out.” ✗
  • Choice B — 'Uranus is a different shade of blue than Neptune?': correct subject-verb order, but the question mark turns the whole sentence into a question — wrong for an indirect question that states a resolved finding. ✗
  • Choice C — 'Uranus is a different shade of blue than Neptune.': normal subject-verb order and a period. This correctly forms an indirect question as a declarative statement inside a larger declarative sentence. ✓
  • Choice D — 'is Uranus a different shade of blue than Neptune.': inverted order violates indirect question grammar even though the period is correct. ✗
That matches choice C — C is correct.
